scene_purpose: Begin Vañci with Kaṇṇaki's own compressed account
emotional_turn: The civic actor becomes a stranger whose history must be received

scene_title: A small settlement receives a deity

emotional_turn: Recognition becomes communal material practice

scene_purpose: Locate worship inside specific ecology
emotional_turn: Kaṇṇaki's memory enters a living mountain system

scene_title: The spear links distinct sacred landscapes

scene_title: Ritual efficacy is debated inside the song
scene_purpose: Show belief as interpreted and contested within community
emotional_turn: Sacred performance becomes social argument

scene_purpose: Complete local moral interpretation of Kaṇṇaki
emotional_turn: A stranger's grief becomes communal Pattini praise

scene_purpose: Bring royal power into an inhabited landscape
emotional_turn: The court becomes listener rather than origin

scene_purpose: Show tribute as political transfer of wealth and knowledge
emotional_turn: Material display reveals labour and asymmetry

scene_title: The Kuravar report what they saw
scene_purpose: Establish local eyewitness authority
emotional_turn: The tribute audience becomes inquiry

scene_purpose: Connect hill testimony to the anklet case and Madurai catastrophe
emotional_turn: The court understands that the moral event is already complete

scene_title: The ruler hears the burden of justice

emotional_turn: Narrative becomes responsibility for the ruler

scene_purpose: Authorise memorial action after local recognition

scene_title: Stone, place and legitimacy are debated

scene_title: A memorial decision becomes an expedition

scene_purpose: Show time, councils, bodies and food behind war
emotional_turn: The vow becomes administrative burden

scene_purpose: Stage sacred and civic legitimation of departure
emotional_turn: Mobilisation becomes public kingship

scene_purpose: Show military movement with ritual obligations
emotional_turn: Expansion encounters communities it must protect or negotiate with

scene_title: Allies and administration sustain the expedition

emotional_turn: Conquest depends on diplomacy and records

scene_purpose: Depict organised mass violence rather than heroic blur
emotional_turn: State logistics become bodily destruction

scene_purpose: Complete the expedition objective while preserving coercion
emotional_turn: Victory converts enemies and landscape into memorial material

scene_title: Victory places weight upon defeated crowns

emotional_turn: The sacred project bears the weight of captive bodies

scene_purpose: Transform expedition material through Ganges consecration
emotional_turn: Victory object becomes ritual material

scene_purpose: Combine ceremonial complex with differentiated martial remembrance
emotional_turn: Triumph is interrupted by dead and wounded bodies

scene_purpose: Make northern victory answer to accumulated grief
emotional_turn: Pukār, Road and Madurai losses enter the victory space

scene_purpose: Move from captive release to plural landscape songs

scene_purpose: Convert expedition into civic memory
emotional_turn: The capital receives a returning ruler and a changed project

scene_purpose: Keep veteran wounds under the triumphal image
emotional_turn: Women receive altered bodies, not abstract victory

scene_purpose: Join civic remission, specialist construction and recurring worship
emotional_turn: Political release precedes durable sacred infrastructure

scene_purpose: Keep women's lived memory prior to transregional royal adoption
emotional_turn: Private grief and diplomatic participation meet without becoming equal

scene_title: The epic closes as ethical instruction
scene_purpose: Return judgement to the audience's conduct
emotional_turn: Monument gives way to truth, restraint, charity and fellowship
